mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings. This healthy economic backdrop typically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth, or the Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power and may prompt the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase interest rates. This tightening monetary stance negatively impacts corporate profitability and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate robust consumer demand and a healthy economy. This synergistic effect typically drives corporate revenue growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when both conditions are met, or the combined signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an aggressive monetary tightening policy. This leads to higher borrowing costs for corporations and a re-evaluation of equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a FEDFUNDS rate hike of this magnitude within a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening demand in the housing sector. This can spill over into broader economic slowdowns, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced construction activity and consumer confidence over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 60% of periods where housing starts fall below the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing and mining sector. This indicates strong supply-side capacity and robust demand for goods, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of sustained INDPRO growth, or the correlation is not statistically significant (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of very low unemployment (UNRATE) and high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) suggests an overheating economy where the Federal Reserve is actively trying to cool demand. This leads to increased recession risk and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where both unemployment is very low and rates are high, or the interaction term is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a strong and expanding economy. This provides a favorable environment for corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of periods following quarters with GDP growth exceeding 3.0%.
